title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 1
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {545} \quad M_{A1B1} = {36.33}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {847.33} \quad n_{A1B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {517} \quad M_{A1B2} = {34.47}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {1029.73} \quad n_{A1B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {457} \quad M_{A2B1} = {30.47}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {917.73} \quad n_{A2B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {560} \quad M_{A2B2} = {37.33}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {547.33} \quad n_{A2B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {613} \quad M_{A3B1} = {40.87}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {1335.73} \quad n_{A3B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {593} \quad M_{A3B2} = {39.53}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {1045.73} \quad n_{A3B2} = {15}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {90} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {89}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {90} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {84}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {126669} - \frac{3285^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {126669} - \frac{10791225}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {126669} - {119902.5}\)  \(SS_{total} = {6766.5}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {847.33 + 1029.73 + 917.73 + 547.33 + 1335.73 + 1045.73}\)  \(SS_{within} = {5723.58}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {6766.5} - {5723.58}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1042.92}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1062}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1017}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1206}  \quad n_{A_3} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.87}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1062}^2}{30} + \frac{{1017}^2}{30} + \frac{{1206}^2}{30}} - \frac{{3285}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1127844}}{30} + \frac{{1034289}}{30} + \frac{{1454436}}{30}} - \frac{{10791225}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{37594.8} + {34476.3} + {48481.2}} - {119902.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {120552.3} - {119902.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {649.8}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{649.8}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {324.9}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{324.9}{68.14}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {4.77}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{649.8}{{6766.5} - {33.62} - {359.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{649.8}{6373.38}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {4.77}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1615}  \quad n_{B_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1670}  \quad n_{B_2} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {6.95}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1615}^2}{45} + \frac{{1670}^2}{45}} - \frac{{3285}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2608225}}{45} + \frac{{2788900}}{45}} - \frac{{10791225}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{57960.56} + {61975.56}} - {119902.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {119936.12} - {119902.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {33.62}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{33.62}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {33.62}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{33.62}{68.14}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.49}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{33.62}{{6766.5} - {649.8} - {359.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{33.62}{5757.2}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {84}) = {0.49}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.87}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1042.92} - {649.8} - {33.62}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {359.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{359.5}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {179.75}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{179.75}{68.14}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.64}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{359.5}{{6766.5} - {649.8} - {33.62}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{359.5}{6083.08}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {2.64}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 2
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {79} \quad M_{A1B1} = {15.8}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {4.8} \quad n_{A1B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {61} \quad M_{A1B2} = {12.2}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {36.8} \quad n_{A1B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {83} \quad M_{A2B1} = {16.6}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {35.2} \quad n_{A2B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {67} \quad M_{A2B2} = {13.4}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {21.2} \quad n_{A2B2} = {5}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {20} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {19}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {20} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {16}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4366} - \frac{290^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4366} - \frac{84100}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4366} - {4205.0}\)  \(SS_{total} = {161.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {4.8 + 36.8 + 35.2 + 21.2}\)  \(SS_{within} = {98.0}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {161.0} - {98.0}\)  \(SS_{between} = {63.0}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {140}  \quad n_{A_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {150}  \quad n_{A_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{140}^2}{10} + \frac{{150}^2}{10}} - \frac{{290}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{19600}}{10} + \frac{{22500}}{10}} - \frac{{84100}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{1960.0} + {2250.0}} - {4205.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {4210.0} - {4205.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {5.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{5.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {5.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{5.0}{6.12}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.82}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5.0}{{161.0} - {57.8} - {0.2}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5.0}{103.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.05}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.82}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.05}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {162}  \quad n_{B_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {128}  \quad n_{B_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{162}^2}{10} + \frac{{128}^2}{10}} - \frac{{290}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{26244}}{10} + \frac{{16384}}{10}} - \frac{{84100}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{2624.4} + {1638.4}} - {4205.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {4262.8} - {4205.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {57.8}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{57.8}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {57.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{57.8}{6.12}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {9.44}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{57.8}{{161.0} - {5.0} - {0.2}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{57.8}{155.8}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.37}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {9.44}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.37}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {63.0} - {5.0} - {57.8}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {0.2}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{0.2}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {0.2}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{0.2}{6.12}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.03}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.2}{{161.0} - {5.0} - {57.8}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.2}{98.2}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.03}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 3
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {749} \quad M_{A1B1} = {49.93}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {400.93} \quad n_{A1B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {653} \quad M_{A1B2} = {43.53}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {529.73} \quad n_{A1B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {692} \quad M_{A1B3} = {46.13}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {931.73} \quad n_{A1B3} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {675} \quad M_{A2B1} = {45.0}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {388.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {500} \quad M_{A2B2} = {33.33}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {513.33} \quad n_{A2B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {480} \quad M_{A2B3} = {32.0}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {350.0} \quad n_{A2B3} = {15}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {90} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {89}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {90} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {84}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {163267} - \frac{3749^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {163267} - \frac{14055001}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {163267} - {156166.68}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7100.32}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {400.93 + 529.73 + 931.73 + 388.0 + 513.33 + 350.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {3113.72}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {7100.32} - {3113.72}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3986.6}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {2094}  \quad n_{A_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1655}  \quad n_{A_2} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {6.95}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2094}^2}{45} + \frac{{1655}^2}{45}} - \frac{{3749}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{4384836}}{45} + \frac{{2739025}}{45}} - \frac{{14055001}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{97440.8} + {60867.22}} - {156166.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {158308.02} - {156166.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {2141.34}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{2141.34}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {2141.34}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{2141.34}{37.07}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {57.76}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2141.34}{{7100.32} - {1525.61} - {319.65}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2141.34}{5255.06}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {84}) = {57.76}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1424}  \quad n_{B_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1153}  \quad n_{B_2} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1172}  \quad n_{B_3} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.87}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1424}^2}{30} + \frac{{1153}^2}{30} + \frac{{1172}^2}{30}} - \frac{{3749}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2027776}}{30} + \frac{{1329409}}{30} + \frac{{1373584}}{30}} - \frac{{14055001}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{67592.53} + {44313.63} + {45786.13}} - {156166.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {157692.29} - {156166.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1525.61}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1525.61}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {762.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{762.8}{37.07}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {20.58}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1525.61}{{7100.32} - {2141.34} - {319.65}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1525.61}{4639.33}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.33}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {20.58}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.33}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.87}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {3986.6} - {2141.34} - {1525.61}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {319.65}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{319.65}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {159.82}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{159.82}{37.07}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.31}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{319.65}{{7100.32} - {2141.34} - {1525.61}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{319.65}{3433.37}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {4.31}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)

Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {169} \quad M_{A1B1} = {16.9}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {22.9} \quad n_{A1B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {198} \quad M_{A1B2} = {19.8}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {115.6} \quad n_{A1B2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {249} \quad M_{A1B3} = {24.9}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {122.9} \quad n_{A1B3} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {161} \quad M_{A2B1} = {16.1}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {82.9} \quad n_{A2B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {222} \quad M_{A2B2} = {22.2}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {113.6} \quad n_{A2B2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {210} \quad M_{A2B3} = {21.0}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {182.0} \quad n_{A2B3} = {10}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {161} \quad M_{A3B1} = {16.1}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {148.9} \quad n_{A3B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {192} \quad M_{A3B2} = {19.2}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {127.6} \quad n_{A3B2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {249} \quad M_{A3B3} = {24.9}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {86.9} \quad n_{A3B3} = {10}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {90} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {89}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {90} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {81}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38389} - \frac{1811^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38389} - \frac{3279721}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38389} - {36441.34}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1947.66}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {22.9 + 115.6 + 122.9 + 82.9 + 113.6 + 182.0 + 148.9 + 127.6 + 86.9}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1003.3}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1947.66} - {1003.3}\)  \(SS_{between} = {944.36}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {616}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {593}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {602}  \quad n_{A_3} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{616}^2}{30} + \frac{{593}^2}{30} + \frac{{602}^2}{30}} - \frac{{1811}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{379456}}{30} + \frac{{351649}}{30} + \frac{{362404}}{30}} - \frac{{3279721}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{12648.53} + {11721.63} + {12080.13}} - {36441.34}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {36450.29} - {36441.34}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {8.95}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{8.95}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {4.47}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{4.47}{12.39}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.36}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{8.95}{{1947.66} - {788.29} - {147.12}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{8.95}{1012.25}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {81}) = {0.36}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {491}  \quad n_{B_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {612}  \quad n_{B_2} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {708}  \quad n_{B_3} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{491}^2}{30} + \frac{{612}^2}{30} + \frac{{708}^2}{30}} - \frac{{1811}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{241081}}{30} + \frac{{374544}}{30} + \frac{{501264}}{30}} - \frac{{3279721}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{8036.03} + {12484.8} + {16708.8}} - {36441.34}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {37229.63} - {36441.34}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {788.29}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{788.29}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {394.14}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{394.14}{12.39}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {31.81}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{788.29}{{1947.66} - {8.95} - {147.12}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{788.29}{1791.59}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.44}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {81}) = {31.81}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.44}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.48}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {944.36} - {8.95} - {788.29}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {147.12}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{147.12}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {36.78}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{36.78}{12.39}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.97}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{147.12}{{1947.66} - {8.95} - {788.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{147.12}{1150.42}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.13}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {81}) = {2.97}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.13}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {71564} - \frac{1914^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {71564} - \frac{3663396}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {71564} - {70449.92}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1114.08}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {100.92 + 214.92 + 331.69 + 217.08}\)  \(SS_{within} = {864.61}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1114.08} - {864.61}\)  \(SS_{between} = {249.47}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {962}  \quad n_{A_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {952}  \quad n_{A_2} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{962}^2}{26} + \frac{{952}^2}{26}} - \frac{{1914}^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{925444}}{26} + \frac{{906304}}{26}} - \frac{{3663396}}{52}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{35594.0} + {34857.85}} - {70449.92}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {70451.85} - {70449.92}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1.93}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1.93}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1.93}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{142.24}{18.01}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {7.9}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{142.24}{{1114.08} - {1.93} - {105.3}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{142.24}{1006.85}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.14}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {7.9}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.14}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {249.47} - {1.93} - {142.24}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {105.3}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{105.3}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {105.3}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{105.3}{18.01}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {5.85}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{105.3}{{1114.08} - {1.93} - {142.24}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{105.3}{969.91}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {5.85}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 6
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {283} \quad M_{A1B1} = {47.17}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {92.83} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {252} \quad M_{A1B2} = {42.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {280.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {261} \quad M_{A2B1} = {43.5}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {893.5} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {299} \quad M_{A2B2} = {49.83}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {346.83} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {334} \quad M_{A3B1} = {55.67}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {321.33} \quad n_{A3B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {341} \quad M_{A3B2} = {56.83}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {158.83} \quad n_{A3B2} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {36} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {35}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {36} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {30}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {90252} - \frac{1770^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {90252} - \frac{3132900}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {90252} - {87025.0}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3227.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {92.83 + 280.0 + 893.5 + 346.83 + 321.33 + 158.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2093.32}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3227.0} - {2093.32}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1133.68}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {535}  \quad n_{A_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {560}  \quad n_{A_2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {675}  \quad n_{A_3} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.39}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{535}^2}{12} + \frac{{560}^2}{12} + \frac{{675}^2}{12}} - \frac{{1770}^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{286225}}{12} + \frac{{313600}}{12} + \frac{{455625}}{12}} - \frac{{3132900}}{36}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{23852.08} + {26133.33} + {37968.75}} - {87025.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {87954.16} - {87025.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {929.16}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{929.16}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {464.58}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{464.58}{69.78}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {6.66}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{929.16}{{3227.0} - {5.45} - {199.07}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{929.16}{3022.48}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {30}) = {6.66}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {878}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {892}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.56}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{878}^2}{18} + \frac{{892}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1770}^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{770884}}{18} + \frac{{795664}}{18}} - \frac{{3132900}}{36}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{42826.89} + {44203.56}} - {87025.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {87030.45} - {87025.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {5.45}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{5.45}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {5.45}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{5.45}{69.78}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.08}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5.45}{{3227.0} - {929.16} - {199.07}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5.45}{2098.77}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {30}) = {0.08}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.39}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1133.68} - {929.16} - {5.45}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {199.07}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{199.07}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {99.54}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{199.07}{{3227.0} - {929.16} - {5.45}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{199.07}{2292.39}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {30}) = {1.43}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {84499} - \frac{1903^2}{45}\)  \(SS_{total} = {84499} - \frac{3621409}{45}\)  \(SS_{total} = {84499} - {80475.76}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4023.24}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {323.2 + 66.0 + 344.8 + 542.8 + 461.2 + 82.8 + 516.8 + 285.2 + 630.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {3253.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {4023.24} - {3253.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = {769.64}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{176.96}{90.38}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.96}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{353.91}{{4023.24} - {12.31} - {403.42}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{353.91}{3607.51}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {1.96}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {643}  \quad n_{B_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {636}  \quad n_{B_2} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {624}  \quad n_{B_3} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{643}^2}{15} + \frac{{636}^2}{15} + \frac{{624}^2}{15}} - \frac{{1903}^2}{45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{413449}}{15} + \frac{{404496}}{15} + \frac{{389376}}{15}} - \frac{{3621409}}{45}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{27563.27} + {26966.4} + {25958.4}} - {80475.76}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {80488.07} - {80475.76}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {12.31}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{12.31}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {6.16}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{6.16}{90.38}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.07}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{12.31}{{4023.24} - {353.91} - {403.42}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{12.31}{3265.91}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {0.07}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.63}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {769.64} - {353.91} - {12.31}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {403.42}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{403.42}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {100.86}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{100.86}{90.38}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {1.12}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{403.42}{{4023.24} - {353.91} - {12.31}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{403.42}{3657.02}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({4}, {36}) = {1.12}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {124598} - \frac{2166^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{total} = {124598} - \frac{4691556}{40}\)  \(SS_{total} = {124598} - {117288.9}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7309.1}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1785.6 + 224.5 + 940.0 + 1260.9}\)  \(SS_{within} = {4211.0}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {7309.1} - {4211.0}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3098.1}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1237}  \quad n_{A_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {929}  \quad n_{A_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1237}^2}{20} + \frac{{929}^2}{20}} - \frac{{2166}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1530169}}{20} + \frac{{863041}}{20}} - \frac{{4691556}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{76508.45} + {43152.05}} - {117288.9}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {119660.5} - {117288.9}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {2371.6}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{2371.6}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {2371.6}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{2371.6}{116.97}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {20.28}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2371.6}{{7309.1} - {624.1} - {102.4}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2371.6}{6582.6}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.36}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {20.28}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.36}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1162}  \quad n_{B_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1004}  \quad n_{B_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1162}^2}{20} + \frac{{1004}^2}{20}} - \frac{{2166}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1350244}}{20} + \frac{{1008016}}{20}} - \frac{{4691556}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{67512.2} + {50400.8}} - {117288.9}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {117913.0} - {117288.9}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {624.1}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{624.1}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {624.1}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{624.1}{116.97}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {5.34}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{624.1}{{7309.1} - {2371.6} - {102.4}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{624.1}{4835.1}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.13}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {5.34}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.13}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {3098.1} - {2371.6} - {624.1}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {102.4}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{102.4}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {102.4}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{102.4}{116.97}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.88}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{102.4}{{7309.1} - {2371.6} - {624.1}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{102.4}{4313.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {0.88}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)

text:

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{8.78}{8.86}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.99}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {68223} - \frac{1407^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {68223} - \frac{1979649}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {68223} - {65988.3}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2234.7}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {152.8 + 98.8 + 22.0 + 202.0 + 58.0 + 272.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {805.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2234.7} - {805.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1429.1}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.61}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1429.1} - {108.3} - {1190.4}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {130.4}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{130.4}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {65.2}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{24.2}{{1321.0} - {561.8} - {9.8}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{24.2}{749.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.53}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {472}  \quad n_{B_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {578}  \quad n_{B_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{472}^2}{10} + \frac{{578}^2}{10}} - \frac{{1050}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{222784}}{10} + \frac{{334084}}{10}} - \frac{{1102500}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{22278.4} + {33408.4}} - {55125.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {55686.8} - {55125.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {561.8}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{561.8}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {561.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{561.8}{45.32}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {12.4}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{561.8}{{1321.0} - {24.2} - {9.8}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{561.8}{1287.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.44}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {12.4}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.44}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {595.8} - {24.2} - {561.8}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {9.8}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{9.8}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {9.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{9.8}{45.32}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.22}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.8}{{1321.0} - {24.2} - {561.8}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.8}{735.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.22}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 12
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {257} \quad M_{A1B1} = {36.71}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {247.43} \quad n_{A1B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {309} \quad M_{A1B2} = {44.14}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {658.86} \quad n_{A1B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {416} \quad M_{A2B1} = {59.43}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {243.71} \quad n_{A2B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {333} \quad M_{A2B2} = {47.57}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {525.71} \quad n_{A2B2} = {7}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {28} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {27}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {28} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {24}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {65315} - \frac{1315^2}{28}\)  \(SS_{total} = {65315} - \frac{1729225}{28}\)  \(SS_{total} = {65315} - {61758.04}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3556.96}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {247.43 + 658.86 + 243.71 + 525.71}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1675.71}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3556.96} - {1675.71}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1881.25}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {566}  \quad n_{A_1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {749}  \quad n_{A_2} = {14}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{566}^2}{14} + \frac{{749}^2}{14}} - \frac{{1315}^2}{28}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{320356}}{14} + \frac{{561001}}{14}} - \frac{{1729225}}{28}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{22882.57} + {40071.5}} - {61758.04}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {62954.07} - {61758.04}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1196.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1196.03}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1196.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1196.03}{69.82}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {17.13}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1196.03}{{3556.96} - {34.32} - {650.9}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1196.03}{2871.74}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.42}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {17.13}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.42}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {673}  \quad n_{B_1} = {14}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {642}  \quad n_{B_2} = {14}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{673}^2}{14} + \frac{{642}^2}{14}} - \frac{{1315}^2}{28}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{452929}}{14} + \frac{{412164}}{14}} - \frac{{1729225}}{28}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{32352.07} + {29440.29}} - {61758.04}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {61792.36} - {61758.04}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {34.32}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{34.32}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {34.32}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{34.32}{69.82}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.49}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{34.32}{{3556.96} - {1196.03} - {650.9}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{34.32}{1710.03}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {0.49}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1881.25} - {1196.03} - {34.32}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {650.9}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{650.9}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {650.9}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{650.9}{69.82}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {9.32}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{650.9}{{3556.96} - {1196.03} - {34.32}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{650.9}{2326.61}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.28}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {9.32}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.28}\)

text:
Problem 13
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {335} \quad M_{A1B1} = {37.22}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {899.56} \quad n_{A1B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {484} \quad M_{A1B2} = {53.78}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {103.56} \quad n_{A1B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {505} \quad M_{A2B1} = {56.11}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {334.89} \quad n_{A2B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {355} \quad M_{A2B2} = {39.44}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {1014.22} \quad n_{A2B2} = {9}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {36} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {35}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {36} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {32}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {83189} - \frac{1679^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {83189} - \frac{2819041}{36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {83189} - {78306.69}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4882.31}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {899.56 + 103.56 + 334.89 + 1014.22}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2352.23}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {4882.31} - {2352.23}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2530.08}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {819}  \quad n_{A_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {860}  \quad n_{A_2} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{819}^2}{18} + \frac{{860}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1679}^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{670761}}{18} + \frac{{739600}}{18}} - \frac{{2819041}}{36}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{37264.5} + {41088.89}} - {78306.69}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {78353.39} - {78306.69}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {46.7}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{46.7}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {46.7}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{46.7}{73.51}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.64}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{46.7}{{4882.31} - {0.03} - {2483.35}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{46.7}{2398.93}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {32}) = {0.64}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {840}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {839}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{840}^2}{18} + \frac{{839}^2}{18}} - \frac{{1679}^2}{36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{705600}}{18} + \frac{{703921}}{18}} - \frac{{2819041}}{36}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{39200.0} + {39106.72}} - {78306.69}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {78306.72} - {78306.69}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {0.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.03}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {0.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.03}{73.51}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.0}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.03}{{4882.31} - {46.7} - {2483.35}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.03}{2352.26}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {32}) = {0.0}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2530.08} - {46.7} - {0.03}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2483.35}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2483.35}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {2483.35}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{2483.35}{73.51}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {33.78}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2483.35}{{4882.31} - {46.7} - {0.03}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2483.35}{4835.58}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.51}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {32}) = {33.78}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.51}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 14
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {275} \quad M_{A1B1} = {21.15}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {193.69} \quad n_{A1B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {194} \quad M_{A1B2} = {14.92}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {148.92} \quad n_{A1B2} = {13}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {311} \quad M_{A1B3} = {23.92}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {266.92} \quad n_{A1B3} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {215} \quad M_{A2B1} = {16.54}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {145.23} \quad n_{A2B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {279} \quad M_{A2B2} = {21.46}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {119.23} \quad n_{A2B2} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {307} \quad M_{A2B3} = {23.62}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {233.08} \quad n_{A2B3} = {13}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {190} \quad M_{A3B1} = {14.62}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {201.08} \quad n_{A3B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {275} \quad M_{A3B2} = {21.15}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {283.69} \quad n_{A3B2} = {13}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {283} \quad M_{A3B3} = {21.77}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {206.31} \quad n_{A3B3} = {13}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {117} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {116}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {117} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {108}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {49499} - \frac{2329^2}{117}\)  \(SS_{total} = {49499} - \frac{5424241}{117}\)  \(SS_{total} = {49499} - {46361.03}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3137.97}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {193.69 + 148.92 + 266.92 + 145.23 + 119.23 + 233.08 + 201.08 + 283.69 + 206.31}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1798.15}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3137.97} - {1798.15}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1339.82}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {780}  \quad n_{A_1} = {39}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {801}  \quad n_{A_2} = {39}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {748}  \quad n_{A_3} = {39}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.08}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{780}^2}{39} + \frac{{801}^2}{39} + \frac{{748}^2}{39}} - \frac{{2329}^2}{117}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{608400}}{39} + \frac{{641601}}{39} + \frac{{559504}}{39}} - \frac{{5424241}}{117}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{15600.0} + {16451.31} + {14346.26}} - {46361.03}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {46397.57} - {46361.03}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {36.54}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{36.54}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {18.27}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{18.27}{16.65}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.1}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{2816.32}{52.36}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {53.79}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{374.99}{{10284.33} - {5632.63} - {1449.31}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{374.99}{3202.39}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.12}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {54}) = {7.16}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.12}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{724.66}{52.36}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {13.84}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1449.31}{{10284.33} - {5632.63} - {374.99}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1449.31}{4276.71}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.34}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {54}) = {13.84}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.34}\)

text:

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {48815} - \frac{1857^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {48815} - \frac{3448449}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {48815} - {47895.12}\)  \(SS_{total} = {919.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {68.67 + 96.92 + 142.25 + 98.92 + 95.67 + 74.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {576.43}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {919.88} - {576.43}\)  \(SS_{between} = {343.45}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{66.13}{8.73}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {7.58}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{66.13}{{919.88} - {211.02} - {66.3}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{66.13}{642.56}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {66}) = {7.58}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {561}  \quad n_{B_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {651}  \quad n_{B_2} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {645}  \quad n_{B_3} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{561}^2}{24} + \frac{{651}^2}{24} + \frac{{645}^2}{24}} - \frac{{1857}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{314721}}{24} + \frac{{423801}}{24} + \frac{{416025}}{24}} - \frac{{3448449}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{13113.38} + {17658.38} + {17334.38}} - {47895.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {48106.14} - {47895.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {211.02}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{211.02}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {105.51}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{105.51}{8.73}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {12.09}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{211.02}{{919.88} - {66.13} - {66.3}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{211.02}{787.45}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.27}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {12.09}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.27}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {343.45} - {66.13} - {211.02}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {66.3}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{66.3}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {33.15}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{33.15}{8.73}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {3.8}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{66.3}{{919.88} - {66.13} - {211.02}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{66.3}{642.73}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {3.8}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\)
