 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2146.69}{{8898.98} - {426.04} - {54.11}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2146.69}{8418.83}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.25}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {42}) = {14.37}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.25}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {609}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {684}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {724}  \quad n_{B_3} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{609}^2}{16} + \frac{{684}^2}{16} + \frac{{724}^2}{16}} - \frac{{2017}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{370881}}{16} + \frac{{467856}}{16} + \frac{{524176}}{16}} - \frac{{4068289}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{23180.06} + {29241.0} + {32761.0}} - {84756.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {85182.06} - {84756.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {426.04}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{426.04}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {213.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{213.02}{149.34}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {1.43}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{426.04}{{8898.98} - {2146.69} - {54.11}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{426.04}{6698.18}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {1.43}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2626.84} - {2146.69} - {426.04}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {54.11}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{54.11}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {27.06}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{27.06}{149.34}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.18}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{54.11}{{8898.98} - {2146.69} - {426.04}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{54.11}{6326.25}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {0.18}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 20
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {231} \quad M_{A1B1} = {23.1}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {150.9} \quad n_{A1B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {178} \quad M_{A1B2} = {17.8}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {95.6} \quad n_{A1B2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {205} \quad M_{A2B1} = {20.5}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {20.5} \quad n_{A2B1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {174} \quad M_{A2B2} = {17.4}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {78.4} \quad n_{A2B2} = {10}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {40} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {39}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {40} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {36}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16080} - \frac{788^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16080} - \frac{620944}{40}\)  \(SS_{total} = {16080} - {15523.6}\)  \(SS_{total} = {556.4}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {150.9 + 95.6 + 20.5 + 78.4}\)  \(SS_{within} = {345.4}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {556.4} - {345.4}\)  \(SS_{between} = {211.0}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {409}  \quad n_{A_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {379}  \quad n_{A_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{409}^2}{20} + \frac{{379}^2}{20}} - \frac{{788}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{167281}}{20} + \frac{{143641}}{20}} - \frac{{620944}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{8364.05} + {7182.05}} - {15523.6}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {15546.1} - {15523.6}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {22.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{22.5}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {22.5}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{22.5}{9.59}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {2.35}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{22.5}{{556.4} - {176.4} - {12.1}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{22.5}{367.9}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {2.35}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {436}  \quad n_{B_1} = {20}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {352}  \quad n_{B_2} = {20}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{436}^2}{20} + \frac{{352}^2}{20}} - \frac{{788}^2}{40}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{190096}}{20} + \frac{{123904}}{20}} - \frac{{620944}}{40}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{9504.8} + {6195.2}} - {15523.6}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {15700.0} - {15523.6}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {176.4}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{176.4}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {176.4}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{176.4}{9.59}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {18.39}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{176.4}{{556.4} - {22.5} - {12.1}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{176.4}{521.8}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.34}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {18.39}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.34}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {211.0} - {22.5} - {176.4}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {12.1}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{12.1}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {12.1}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{12.1}{9.59}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {1.26}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{12.1}{{556.4} - {22.5} - {176.4}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{12.1}{357.5}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {1.26}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 21
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {541} \quad M_{A1B1} = {49.18}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {201.64} \quad n_{A1B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {561} \quad M_{A1B2} = {51.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {620.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {667} \quad M_{A2B1} = {60.64}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {272.55} \quad n_{A2B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {479} \quad M_{A2B2} = {43.55}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {340.73} \quad n_{A2B2} = {11}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {44} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {43}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {44} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {40}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {117956} - \frac{2248^2}{44}\)  \(SS_{total} = {117956} - \frac{5053504}{44}\)  \(SS_{total} = {117956} - {114852.36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3103.64}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {201.64 + 620.0 + 272.55 + 340.73}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1434.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3103.64} - {1434.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1668.72}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1102}  \quad n_{A_1} = {22}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1146}  \quad n_{A_2} = {22}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.08}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1102}^2}{22} + \frac{{1146}^2}{22}} - \frac{{2248}^2}{44}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1214404}}{22} + \frac{{1313316}}{22}} - \frac{{5053504}}{44}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{55200.18} + {59696.18}} - {114852.36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {114896.36} - {114852.36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {44.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{44.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {44.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{44.0}{35.87}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.23}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{44.0}{{3103.64} - {641.46} - {983.26}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{44.0}{1478.92}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {40}) = {1.23}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1208}  \quad n_{B_1} = {22}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1040}  \quad n_{B_2} = {22}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.08}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1208}^2}{22} + \frac{{1040}^2}{22}} - \frac{{2248}^2}{44}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1459264}}{22} + \frac{{1081600}}{22}} - \frac{{5053504}}{44}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{66330.18} + {49163.64}} - {114852.36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {115493.82} - {114852.36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {641.46}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{641.46}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {641.46}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{641.46}{35.87}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {17.88}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{641.46}{{3103.64} - {44.0} - {983.26}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{641.46}{2076.38}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {40}) = {17.88}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.08}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1668.72} - {44.0} - {641.46}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {983.26}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{983.26}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {983.26}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{983.26}{35.87}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {27.41}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{983.26}{{3103.64} - {44.0} - {641.46}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{983.26}{2418.18}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {40}) = {27.41}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.41}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 22
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {530} \quad M_{A1B1} = {66.25}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {707.5} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {579} \quad M_{A1B2} = {72.38}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {981.88} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {477} \quad M_{A2B1} = {59.62}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {359.88} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {412} \quad M_{A2B2} = {51.5}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {2038.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {32} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {31}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {32} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {28}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {130764} - \frac{1998^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {130764} - \frac{3992004}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {130764} - {124750.12}\)  \(SS_{total} = {6013.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {707.5 + 981.88 + 359.88 + 2038.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {4087.26}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {6013.88} - {4087.26}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1926.62}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1512.5}{145.97}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {10.36}\)

 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.1}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {105.01} - {28.16} - {16.66}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {60.19}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{60.19}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {60.19}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{60.19}{40.72}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {1.48}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{60.19}{{919.33} - {28.16} - {16.66}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{60.19}{874.51}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.07}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {1.48}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.07}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {12792} - \frac{498^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {12792} - \frac{248004}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {12792} - {12400.2}\)  \(SS_{total} = {391.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {38.8 + 27.2 + 16.0 + 36.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {118.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {391.8} - {118.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = {273.0}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {247}  \quad n_{A_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {251}  \quad n_{A_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{247}^2}{10} + \frac{{251}^2}{10}} - \frac{{498}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{61009}}{10} + \frac{{63001}}{10}} - \frac{{248004}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{6100.9} + {6300.1}} - {12400.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {12401.0} - {12400.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {0.8}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{0.8}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {0.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{0.8}{7.42}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.11}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.8}{{391.8} - {80.0} - {192.2}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.8}{119.6}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.11}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {229}  \quad n_{B_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {269}  \quad n_{B_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{229}^2}{10} + \frac{{269}^2}{10}} - \frac{{498}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{52441}}{10} + \frac{{72361}}{10}} - \frac{{248004}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{5244.1} + {7236.1}} - {12400.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {12480.2} - {12400.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {80.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{80.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {80.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{80.0}{7.42}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {10.78}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{80.0}{{391.8} - {0.8} - {192.2}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{80.0}{198.8}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.4}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {10.78}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.4}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {273.0} - {0.8} - {80.0}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {192.2}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{192.2}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {192.2}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{192.2}{7.42}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {25.9}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{192.2}{{391.8} - {0.8} - {80.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{192.2}{311.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.62}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {25.9}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.62}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {155766} - \frac{2616^2}{45}\)  \(SS_{total} = {155766} - \frac{6843456}{45}\)  \(SS_{total} = {155766} - {152076.8}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3689.2}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {98.8 + 120.8 + 204.0 + 34.0 + 173.2 + 124.8 + 144.8 + 161.2 + 350.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1412.4}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3689.2} - {1412.4}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2276.8}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {925}  \quad n_{A_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {819}  \quad n_{A_2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {872}  \quad n_{A_3} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.25}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{925}^2}{15} + \frac{{819}^2}{15} + \frac{{872}^2}{15}} - \frac{{2616}^2}{45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{855625}}{15} + \frac{{670761}}{15} + \frac{{760384}}{15}} - \frac{{6843456}}{45}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{57041.67} + {44717.4} + {50692.27}} - {152076.8}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {152451.34} - {152076.8}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {374.54}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{374.54}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {187.27}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{187.27}{39.23}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {4.77}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{374.54}{{3689.2} - {1195.6} - {706.66}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{374.54}{1786.94}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.21}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {4.77}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.21}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {810}  \quad n_{B_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {981}  \quad n_{B_2} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {825}  \quad n_{B_3} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.25}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{810}^2}{15} + \frac{{981}^2}{15} + \frac{{825}^2}{15}} - \frac{{2616}^2}{45}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{656100}}{15} + \frac{{962361}}{15} + \frac{{680625}}{15}} - \frac{{6843456}}{45}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{43740.0} + {64157.4} + {45375.0}} - {152076.8}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {153272.4} - {152076.8}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1195.6}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1195.6}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {597.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{597.8}{39.23}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {15.24}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1195.6}{{3689.2} - {374.54} - {706.66}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1195.6}{2608.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.46}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {15.24}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.46}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.89}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2276.8} - {374.54} - {1195.6}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {706.66}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{706.66}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {176.66}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{176.66}{39.23}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.5}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{706.66}{{3689.2} - {374.54} - {1195.6}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{706.66}{2119.06}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.33}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {36}) = {4.5}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.33}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {93166} - \frac{3258^2}{117}\)  \(SS_{total} = {93166} - \frac{10614564}{117}\)  \(SS_{total} = {93166} - {90722.77}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2443.23}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {115.23 + 68.77 + 203.08 + 72.31 + 46.92 + 146.31 + 151.69 + 130.92 + 108.77}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1044.0}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2443.23} - {1044.0}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1399.23}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1016}  \quad n_{A_1} = {39}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1164}  \quad n_{A_2} = {39}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1078}  \quad n_{A_3} = {39}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.08}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1016}^2}{39} + \frac{{1164}^2}{39} + \frac{{1078}^2}{39}} - \frac{{3258}^2}{117}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1032256}}{39} + \frac{{1354896}}{39} + \frac{{1162084}}{39}} - \frac{{10614564}}{117}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{26468.1} + {34740.92} + {29797.03}} - {90722.77}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {91006.05} - {90722.77}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {283.28}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{283.28}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {141.64}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{141.64}{9.67}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {14.65}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{283.28}{{2443.23} - {404.66} - {711.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{283.28}{1327.28}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.21}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {108}) = {14.65}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.21}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1051}  \quad n_{B_1} = {39}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1020}  \quad n_{B_2} = {39}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1187}  \quad n_{B_3} = {39}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.08}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1051}^2}{39} + \frac{{1020}^2}{39} + \frac{{1187}^2}{39}} - \frac{{3258}^2}{117}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1104601}}{39} + \frac{{1040400}}{39} + \frac{{1408969}}{39}} - \frac{{10614564}}{117}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{28323.1} + {26676.92} + {36127.41}} - {90722.77}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {91127.43} - {90722.77}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {404.66}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{404.66}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {202.33}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{202.33}{9.67}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {20.92}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{404.66}{{2443.23} - {283.28} - {711.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{404.66}{1448.66}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.28}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {108}) = {20.92}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.28}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.46}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1399.23} - {283.28} - {404.66}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {711.29}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{711.29}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {177.82}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{177.82}{9.67}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {18.39}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{711.29}{{2443.23} - {283.28} - {404.66}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{711.29}{1755.29}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.41}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {108}) = {18.39}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.41}\)

\(T_{A} = {763} \quad SS_{A} = {690.92}\)  \(T_{B} = {761} \quad SS_{B} = {698.92}\)  \(T_{C} = {497} \quad SS_{C} = {754.92}\)  \(T_{D} = {470} \quad SS_{D} = {1139.67}\)  \(T_{E} = {828} \quad SS_{E} = {464.0}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.54}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {60} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {59}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {60} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {55}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {196647} - \frac{3319^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {196647} - \frac{11015761}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {196647} - {183596.02}\]  \[SS_{total} = {13050.98}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {690.92 + 698.92 + 754.92 + 1139.67 + 464.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = {3748.43}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {13050.98} - {3748.43}\]  \[SS_{between} = {9302.55}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{9302.55}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {2325.64}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{3748.43}{55}\]  \[MS_{within} = {68.15}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{2325.64}{68.15}\]  \[F_{obt} = {34.13}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{9302.55}{13050.98}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.71}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {55}) = {34.13}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.71}\)

The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {25}) = {24.12}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.79}\)

Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{9.49}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {4.74}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{85.26}{33}\]  \[MS_{within} = {2.58}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{4.74}{2.58}\]  \[F_{obt} = {1.84}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{9.49}{94.75}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.1}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {33}) = {1.84}, p > {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.1}\)

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {20} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {19}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {20} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {16}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {33262} - \frac{788^2}{20}\]  \[SS_{total} = {33262} - \frac{620944}{20}\]  \[SS_{total} = {33262} - {31047.2}\]  \[SS_{total} = {2214.8}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {564.8 + 14.8 + 204.8 + 117.2}\]  \[SS_{within} = {901.6}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {2214.8} - {901.6}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1313.2}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{1313.2}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {437.73}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{901.6}{16}\]  \[MS_{within} = {56.35}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{437.73}{56.35}\]  \[F_{obt} = {7.77}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{1313.2}{2214.8}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.59}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {16}) = {7.77}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.59}\)

Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{160.21}{2983.22}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.05}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {33}) = {0.94}, p > {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.05}\)

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {2694} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {126624} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {60}\) 
\(T_{A} = {791} \quad SS_{A} = {876.93}\)  \(T_{B} = {659} \quad SS_{B} = {342.93}\)  \(T_{C} = {502} \quad SS_{C} = {413.73}\)  \(T_{D} = {742} \quad SS_{D} = {821.73}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {60} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {59}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {60} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {56}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {126624} - \frac{2694^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {126624} - \frac{7257636}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {126624} - {120960.6}\]  \[SS_{total} = {5663.4}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {876.93 + 342.93 + 413.73 + 821.73}\]  \[SS_{within} = {2455.32}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {5663.4} - {2455.32}\]  \[SS_{between} = {3208.08}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{3208.08}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {1069.36}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{2455.32}{56}\]  \[MS_{within} = {43.85}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{1069.36}{43.85}\]  \[F_{obt} = {24.39}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{3208.08}{5663.4}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.57}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {56}) = {24.39}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.57}\)

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {50.86}, M_B = {35.07}\] \[SS_A = {265.71}, SS_B = {128.93}\] \[n_A = {14}, n_B = {14}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.78}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {26}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{265.71 + 128.93}{13 + 13}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{394.64}{26}\]  \[s_p^2 = {15.18}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{15.18}{14} + \frac{15.18}{14}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{1.08 + 1.08}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{2.16}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {1.47}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(50.86 - 35.07) - {0}}{1.47}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{15.79}{1.47}\]  \[t_{obt} = {10.74}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(50.86 - 35.07)}{{{\sqrt{15.18}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(15.79)}{3.9}\]  \[d = {4.05}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(26) = 10.74, p < 0.01, d = 4.05

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(23.29 - 28.07) - {0}}{1.67}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-4.78}{1.67}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-2.86}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(23.29 - 28.07)}{{{\sqrt{19.45}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-4.78)}{4.41}\]  \[d = {-1.08}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(26) = -2.86, p < 0.01, d = -1.08

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(23.07 - 27.07) - {0}}{1.7}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-4.0}{1.7}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-2.35}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(23.07 - 27.07)}{{{\sqrt{20.3}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-4.0)}{4.51}\]  \[d = {-0.89}\]

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(55.86 - 64.29) - {0}}{2.36}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-8.43}{2.36}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-3.57}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(55.86 - 64.29)}{{{\sqrt{38.87}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-8.43)}{6.23}\]  \[d = {-1.35}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(26) = -3.57, p < 0.01, d = -1.35

\(T_{A} = {634} \quad SS_{A} = {131.67}\)  \(T_{B} = {519} \quad SS_{B} = {234.25}\)  \(T_{C} = {774} \quad SS_{C} = {355.0}\)  \(T_{D} = {539} \quad SS_{D} = {270.92}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.82}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {48} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {47}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {48} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {44}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {131068} - \frac{2466^2}{48}\]  \[SS_{total} = {131068} - \frac{6081156}{48}\]  \[SS_{total} = {131068} - {126690.75}\]  \[SS_{total} = {4377.25}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {131.67 + 234.25 + 355.0 + 270.92}\]  \[SS_{within} = {991.84}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {4377.25} - {991.84}\]  \[SS_{between} = {3385.41}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{3385.41}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {1128.47}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{991.84}{44}\]  \[MS_{within} = {22.54}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{1128.47}{22.54}\]  \[F_{obt} = {50.07}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{3385.41}{4377.25}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.77}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {44}) = {50.07}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.77}\)

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Two-Factor ANOVA
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {629} \quad M_{A1B1} = {41.93}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {1142.93} \quad n_{A1B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {702} \quad M_{A1B2} = {46.8}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {1602.4} \quad n_{A1B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {551} \quad M_{A1B3} = {36.73}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {954.93} \quad n_{A1B3} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {746} \quad M_{A2B1} = {49.73}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {1236.93} \quad n_{A2B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {657} \quad M_{A2B2} = {43.8}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {2082.4} \quad n_{A2B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {508} \quad M_{A2B3} = {33.87}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {857.73} \quad n_{A2B3} = {15}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {90} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {89}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {90} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {84}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {170429} - \frac{3793^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {170429} - \frac{14386849}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {170429} - {159853.88}\)  \(SS_{total} = {10575.12}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1142.93 + 1602.4 + 954.93 + 1236.93 + 2082.4 + 857.73}\)  \(SS_{within} = {7877.32}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {10575.12} - {7877.32}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2697.8}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1882}  \quad n_{A_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1911}  \quad n_{A_2} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {6.95}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1882}^2}{45} + \frac{{1911}^2}{45}} - \frac{{3793}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{3541924}}{45} + \frac{{3651921}}{45}} - \frac{{14386849}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{78709.42} + {81153.8}} - {159853.88}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {159863.22} - {159853.88}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {9.34}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{9.34}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {9.34}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{9.34}{93.78}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.1}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.34}{{10575.12} - {2112.35} - {576.11}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.34}{7886.66}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {84}) = {0.1}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1375}  \quad n_{B_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1359}  \quad n_{B_2} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1059}  \quad n_{B_3} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.87}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1375}^2}{30} + \frac{{1359}^2}{30} + \frac{{1059}^2}{30}} - \frac{{3793}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1890625}}{30} + \frac{{1846881}}{30} + \frac{{1121481}}{30}} - \frac{{14386849}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{63020.83} + {61562.7} + {37382.7}} - {159853.88}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {161966.23} - {159853.88}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {2112.35}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{2112.35}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {1056.18}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{1056.18}{93.78}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {11.26}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2112.35}{{10575.12} - {9.34} - {576.11}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2112.35}{9989.67}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.21}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {11.26}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.21}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.87}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2697.8} - {9.34} - {2112.35}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {576.11}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{576.11}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {288.06}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{288.06}{93.78}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {3.07}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{576.11}{{10575.12} - {9.34} - {2112.35}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{576.11}{8453.43}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.07}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {3.07}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.07}\)

title: Hypothesis Testing with z-Scores
text:
Problem 1
Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the population mean: \(\mu = {48}\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)





A




50


62


74


59


61


63


69


70


79


50


52


67


47



The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {61.77}\] \[{\sigma} = {10}\] \[n = {13}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 48\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 48\] 
The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = \pm{2.58}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{10}{\sqrt{13}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{10}{3.61}\]  \[\sigma_M = {2.77}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{61.77 - 48}{2.77}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{13.77}{2.77}\]  \[z_{obt} = {4.97}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{61.77 - 48}{10}\]  \[d = \frac{13.77}{10}\]  \[d = {1.38}\]  The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  z = 4.97, p < 0.01, d = 1.38

New Summary statistics for the difference scores: \[M_D = {-0.12}, \quad \Sigma{D} = {-1}, \quad \Sigma{D^2} = {1623}\] 
Calculate SS of the difference scores  \[SS_D = \Sigma D^2 - \frac{(\Sigma D)^2}{n}\]  \[SS_D = {1623} - \frac{1}{8}\]  \[SS_D = {1623} - {0.12}\]  \[SS_D = {1622.88}\] 
Calculate the variance of the difference scores  \[s_D^2 = \frac{SS_D}{df}\]  \[s_D^2 = \frac{1622.88}{7}\]  \[s_D^2 = {231.84}\] 
Calculate the estimated standard error of the difference scores  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{231.84}{8}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{28.98}\]  \[s_{M_D} = {5.38}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-0.12 - 0}{5.38}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-0.12}{5.38}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-0.02}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{-0.12}{{{\sqrt{231.84}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{-0.12}{15.23}\]  \[d = {-0.01}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(7) = -0.02, p > 0.05, d = -0.01

Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {28} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {27}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {28} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {24}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {58860} - \frac{1244^2}{28}\]  \[SS_{total} = {58860} - \frac{1547536}{28}\]  \[SS_{total} = {58860} - {55269.14}\]  \[SS_{total} = {3590.86}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {238.86 + 1079.43 + 326.86 + 530.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = {2175.15}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {3590.86} - {2175.15}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1415.71}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {7} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {6}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {24} - {6}\]  \[df_{error} = {18}\]

\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{1368.01}{30}\]  \[MS_{error} = {45.6}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{10.33}{45.6}\]  \[F_{obt} = {0.23}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{30.99}{{1701.0} - {302.0}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{30.99}{1399.0}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({3}, {30}) = {0.23}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{88^2}{3} + \frac{102^2}{3} + \frac{87^2}{3} + \frac{95^2}{3} + \frac{85^2}{3}} - \frac{457^2}{15}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{7744}{3} + \frac{10404}{3} + \frac{7569}{3} + \frac{9025}{3} + \frac{7225}{3}} - \frac{208849}{15}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{2581.33} + {3468.0} + {2523.0} + {3008.33} + {2408.33}} - {13923.27}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {13988.99} - {13923.27}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {65.72}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {237.6} - {65.72}\]  \[SS_{error} = {171.88}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{176.13}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {88.06}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{171.88}{8}\]  \[MS_{error} = {21.48}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{88.06}{21.48}\]  \[F_{obt} = {4.1}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{176.13}{{413.73} - {65.72}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{176.13}{348.01}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.51}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {8}) = {4.1}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.51}\)
